title: Rename Bug → Issue across code, spec, docs, CLI
body:
# Goal

Rename "bug" to "issue" everywhere in jjforge. Match
git-bug/Gitea/GitHub/Beads on terminology; "bug" suggests
defects, but jjforge tracks epics, research, work items —
issues, not just bugs. End state: `BugId` → `IssueId`,
`bugs/<id>.json` → `issues/<id>.json`, `bugs` bookmark →
`issues` bookmark, CLI help text reads "issue" throughout,
spec uses "issue."

# Why now

The README rewrite (`84343a8`+) surfaced this: the project
calls its tracked artifacts "bugs" everywhere in code, but
nothing the project currently tracks is a defect. Every open
item is an epic or a roadmap. New readers reading
`jjf new` create what?` will trip on "bug."

The terminology gap also affects future external consumers
of jjforge. Beads, Gitea, GitHub, Forgejo all call them
"issues." If jjforge ships its v1 with "bug" baked in, every
future user has to mentally translate.

The v1 spec hasn't shipped publicly yet. The rename cost is
bounded and now is when it's cheapest.

# Scope

## Code (the load-bearing rename)

- `crates/jjf-storage/`:
  - `BugId` → `IssueId`. Re-export and update every reference.
  - `BugRecord` → `IssueRecord`. `BugDraft` → `IssueDraft`.
    `Bug` → `Issue`.
  - `bug_json_relpath` / `bug_comments_relpath` → `issue_*`.
  - `Storage::create_bug` → `create_issue`. `read`,
    `read_history`, `set_status`, `set_title`, `set_body`,
    `set_assignee`, `add_label`, `remove_label`,
    `add_dependency`, `remove_dependency`, `add_comment`,
    `list_ids`, `update`, `bugs_heads` → `issues_heads`,
    `record_merge` (unchanged), `record_merge_op_space`.
    Any param named `bug_id` or `bug` → `issue_id` / `issue`.
  - `Op` variants (`Op::CommentAdd { bug_id, … }`, etc.):
    rename payload field `bug_id` → `issue_id`. **Spec
    impact:** trailer JSON payloads carry `bug_id` today;
    bumping to `issue_id` is a wire-format change. Either
    (a) parse both, emit `issue_id` (forward-compat), or
    (b) hard-cut and rev the spec to v2. Pick during impl;
    (a) is the lower-risk default.
  - Error variants (`Error::BugNotFound` → `IssueNotFound`,
    `MissingBugsBookmark` → `MissingIssuesBookmark`).
  - `BUGS_BOOKMARK` constant → `ISSUES_BOOKMARK`. Bookmark
    name on the underlying jj repo changes from `bugs` to
    `issues`. **This means existing repos need to rename
    the bookmark** — small one-time migration step.

- `crates/jjf-merge/`:
  - Crate name stays (it's a merge driver, not bug-specific)
    but internal docstrings and field references update.

- `crates/jjf/`:
  - CLI verb names stay (`new`, `show`, `ls`, etc. don't
    say "bug" anywhere on the command line).
  - Help text everywhere: "bug" → "issue."
  - `CliError::BugNotFound` → `IssueNotFound`, etc. Mirror
    the storage rename.
  - `kind` strings in `--json` error envelopes:
    `bug_not_found` → `issue_not_found`,
    `missing_bugs_bookmark` → `missing_issues_bookmark`.
    **Wire-format change** — anyone parsing the JSON
    envelope today (`docs/cli-json.md` consumers) sees a
    breaking rename. Document in `cli-json.md` changelog
    section.
  - All test names and assertions referencing "bug" rename.

- `bugs/` directory at storage layer → `issues/`.

## Spec and docs

- `docs/storage-format.md`: every occurrence of "bug" → "issue,"
  `bugs/<id>.json` → `issues/<id>.json`, `bugs` bookmark →
  `issues` bookmark, trailer field `bug_id` → `issue_id`.
  Rev to v2 if going hard-cut on trailer field; keep v1 if
  going forward-compat parse. Add a "v1 → v2 migration"
  appendix either way: the bookmark rename and (if
  applicable) trailer field rename.

- `docs/cli-json.md`: "bug" → "issue," `kind` rename table.
  Changelog entry noting the rename break.

- `docs/git-bug-cutover.md`: this doc is historical; the
  pre-cutover data was in git-bug ("bug" was its term).
  Don't rename retroactively — the doc reads "git-bug
  bugs" naturally. Add one note: "After the Issue rename
  (`<id>`), the project's CURRENT artifacts are called
  issues; the archived data in `refs/bugs/*` is still
  git-bug bugs."

- `CLAUDE.md`: "bug" → "issue" throughout. Especially:
  "Creating a new bug" → "Creating a new issue."
  "Issue-id length" stays (already says "issue"). Queries
  section, orchestration sections, subagent discipline.

- `README.md`: already going through a rewrite (will be
  written with "issue" from the start; verify post-rename
  that nothing slipped back to "bug").

## Migration (this repo)

- The current `bugs` bookmark in this repo has 7 issues on
  it. Migration: `jj bookmark create issues -r bookmarks(bugs)`
  + `jj bookmark delete bugs` (or rename if jj supports
  it directly). Verify `jjf ls` still finds them under
  the new bookmark.
- Storage spec migration: existing `bugs/<id>.json` files
  on the bookmark need renaming to `issues/<id>.json`.
  Same with `.comments.jsonl`. This is a one-time
  rewrite. **Probably best done as a small migration
  command (`jjf migrate v1-to-v2`) that does the bookmark
  rename + file rename in one commit on the new bookmark.**
  Or inline the migration into `Storage::open` —
  detect-and-rename on first call. **Pick during impl;
  the inline-detect path is friendlier for external
  consumers who might never run a migration command.**

## Subagent skill

The `subagent-working-a-git-bug-issue` skill already needs
a rename post-cutover; this rename gives it the right name
on the first try. New name: `subagent-working-a-jjforge-issue`.

# Acceptance

- [ ] All Rust types renamed; `cargo build` clean.
- [ ] All `cargo nextest run --workspace` tests pass under
  the new names.
- [ ] Spec `docs/storage-format.md` updated; trailer field
  rename decision recorded (forward-compat parse vs hard
  cut).
- [ ] `docs/cli-json.md` updated with new error kinds and
  a changelog entry.
- [ ] `CLAUDE.md` and `README.md` swept for stale "bug"
  references.
- [ ] This repo's `bugs` bookmark migrated to `issues`;
  `jjf ls` still finds the 7 issues.
- [ ] Migration path documented for external consumers
  (either a `jjf migrate` command or inline-detect).
- [ ] `bin/` directory note: the shim doesn't exist
  anyway (was deleted; see surprise note from cutover
  round). Don't try to rename a thing that isn't there.
- [ ] Closing comment lists every wire-format / spec
  change so consumers know what they're updating against.

# Out of scope

- Renaming `jjf-merge` crate (it's a merge driver, not an
  issue tracker).
- Renaming the binary `jjf` (the binary name doesn't say
  "bug").
- Renaming `BugId`'s underlying 7-hex random allocation
  (the algorithm and id space don't change).
- A `jjf bug` aliased verb that maps to `jjf new` (we
  don't have CLI verbs that say "bug" today).

# Pointers

- Storage: `crates/jjf-storage/src/lib.rs`, `id.rs`,
  `record.rs`, `op.rs`, `read.rs`, `history.rs`,
  `trailer.rs`, `merge_ops.rs`.
- Spec: `docs/storage-format.md` (rev to v2 if hard-cut).
- CLI: `crates/jjf/src/main.rs`, `preflight.rs`.
- JSON contract: `docs/cli-json.md`.
- CLAUDE.md: this file.
- README: project root.
- Prior rename precedent: the cutover round renamed
  git-bug entities → jjforge entities at the data level,
  not at the type-system level. This is the type-system
  pass.
